title: Planning UX + manifest ownership + graph-driven epic execution
description:
## User story

As the user of @oisincoveney/dev, I want the harness to (1) surface a feature-domain options menu after every multi-file intent so I never type slash commands, (2) own the files it ships through a hash-verified manifest so update preserves my customizations and removes retired files cleanly, (3) treat each bd ticket as an atomic unit of work — claim → implement → verify → commit → close — with parallel fan-out via git worktrees when I opt in, and (4) self-extend the work via a verifier loop that files NEW bd tickets when it finds issues outside the current ticket's acceptance criteria.

## Two concerns, one release (0.8.0)

This epic spans two distinct concerns, both shipped together:

- **Package-install lifecycle** (Phases A + E) — manifest-based ownership, drift detection, retirement of old shipped files. Touches `src/install.ts`, `src/update.ts`, `src/init.ts`. Affects `oisin-dev init` / `oisin-dev update` behavior in downstream consumers.
- **bd workflow** (Phases B + C + D) — planning UX (menu + grill-me + to-bd-issues), edit-boundary gates, atomic-execution skills, verifier with code-review + skill-aware selection, self-repeating ticket-discovery loop. Touches `templates/skills/`, `templates/rules/`, `templates/hooks/`. Affects how an agent works inside a downstream consumer's project.

The two share infrastructure (the package SHIPS the bd workflow rules / hooks / skills) but the codepaths and test surfaces are independent.

## Success Criteria

1. WHEN the user states intent for non-trivial work AND no `in_progress` claim exists THEN the agent SHALL surface a menu of 2–4 feature-domain options plus `Grill me` plus standard process overrides (`Chat`, `Just do it`, `Defer`), rendered as a markdown list by default OR via AskUserQuestion when the chip UI fits the context. Options are about the actual feature/problem, never about ticket structure.

2. WHEN the user picks `Grill me` THEN the agent SHALL invoke the vendored grill-me skill (one-question-at-a-time, recommended-answer-included) until the design tree is resolved, then re-surface a fresh options menu.

3. WHEN the user picks a structural option THEN the agent SHALL draft the EARS epic body + child tickets in conversation, show them, accept redirects, and only run `bd create --type=epic` after explicit user approval.

4. WHEN `oisin-dev init` runs AND prior install artifacts exist THEN the agent SHALL: read prior manifest, hash-check each tracked file, replace clean files silently, back up mildly-drifted files to `<path>.user-backup`, prompt interactively for super-drifted files (line-count delta >25% OR >20 absolute added lines), halt-and-prompt for any drift in `lefthook.yml`, and emit an end-of-init summary listing every `.user-backup` created.

5. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs AND a tracked file has drifted THEN the agent SHALL write `<path>.dev-new` next to the user's file with a one-line warning per drift. Lefthook drift halts the update with a diff and refuses to proceed without explicit acknowledgment.

6. WHEN a tracked file is dropped from the new manifest (retired) THEN the agent SHALL remove it on init AND update, applying the same drift rule (drifted-retired files become `.user-backup` instead of being deleted).

7. WHEN the user claims a bd ticket whose parent epic has no swarm registered AND `tools` includes `'beads'` THEN `require-swarm.sh` SHALL deny Edit/Write until `bd swarm create <epic>` is run.

8. WHEN the agent commits via `git commit` THEN lefthook commit-msg SHALL reject commits without `(<bd-id>)` in scope OR `Refs: <bd-id>` in footer.

9. WHEN the agent finishes work for a claimed ticket THEN it SHALL invoke a fresh-context verifier subagent that:
   - (a) loads the `code-review` skill as the primary review framework
   - (b) loads additional skills based on the diff: `architecture` (multi-layer changes), `testing-strategy` (test surfaces touched), `security-review` (auth / input handling / secrets touched), `accessibility` (UI touched), `performance` (hot-path code touched), `tech-debt` (always)
   - (c) re-reads `bd show <id>` and the diff
   - (d) checks each EARS acceptance criterion + scope vs `Files Likely Touched`
   - (e) for any issue found that is OUTSIDE the original AC, files a NEW bd ticket via `bd create --type=task --deps=discovered-from:<current> --priority=N`
   - (f) returns PASS / PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS / PARTIAL / FAIL with per-criterion evidence

10. WHEN the verifier returns PASS or PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS THEN the main agent SHALL run `bd close <current>` and automatically claim the next ready issue. The newly-filed followups (from 9e) appear in `bd ready`, completing the self-repeating loop. Loop terminates when the verifier returns clean PASS with zero new tickets created.

11. WHEN the verifier returns PARTIAL or FAIL THEN the main agent SHALL fix the failing items in the current ticket, do NOT call `bd close`, then re-run the verifier.

12. WHEN the user picks "fan out" from a menu and the active swarm has ≥2 ready fronts THEN the agent SHALL spawn parallel sub-agents with `isolation: worktree` (capped at 3 concurrent), each scoped to a single ticket.

13. WHEN scope expands during work (file outside the claimed ticket's `Files Likely Touched`) THEN the agent SHALL `bd create --type=task --deps=discovered-from:<current>` automatically, log a one-line note, and stay scoped to the current ticket.

14. WHEN this epic closes THEN `applyDeleteriousMigrations` and `pruneRetiredHookFiles` SHALL be removed (replaced by manifest-driven removal). The `package.json` version SHALL be 0.8.0.

## Out of Scope

- Mechanical enforcement that the agent invoked grill-me before filing an epic (intentional — grill-me is opt-in by user via menu).
- Symbol-existence checking for non-TypeScript languages (still deferred from the prior epic).
- Cross-tool migration (downstream consumers on Cursor / Codex get the same generated files but the menu UX is Claude-Code-specific for this round).
- Auto-detecting "fan out is worth it" — fan-out is always user-opt-in via menu.

## Constitution references

- bd is the single source of truth for specs/plans/research/decisions.
- Planning is collaborative; the agent never unilaterally files epics.
- The agent presents options; the user picks.
- One ticket per atomic unit of work; one commit per ticket close.
- Verifier uses code-review skill + skill-aware selection; verifier-found issues spawn new tickets (self-repeating loop) instead of being silently fixed inline.
- TDD per existing rules (red → green → refactor).
- No follow-up questions; verification before completion claims.

title: Update flow handles deleterious changes from 0.6.5 → 0.7.0 gracefully
description:
## User story

As a downstream consumer running `oisin-dev update` after upgrading from 0.6.5 to 0.7.0, I want all retired files / removed config fields / now-conflicting markdown blocks cleaned up automatically, so that I do not end up with a half-migrated project that has stale slash commands, dead schema keys, or conflicting integration blocks.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs THEN `.claude/commands/spec.md` SHALL be removed if present (legacy, never regenerated in 0.7.0).
2. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs THEN `.claude/specs/TEMPLATE.md` SHALL be removed if present (legacy template, never regenerated). The parent `.claude/specs/` directory SHALL be removed if it ends up empty after the TEMPLATE.md removal; otherwise a warning SHALL be logged listing the remaining files.
3. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs AND `.claude/plans/` exists THEN a warning SHALL be logged listing the contained files (don't auto-delete user-authored plans). Same for `docs/research/`.
4. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs THEN `trimBeadsIntegrationBlock` SHALL run on AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md regardless of whether bd-related side effects fire (it currently only runs inside `configureBeadsAfterInit`, which is gated on `skipSideEffects=false`).
5. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs AND `.dev.config.json` contains the now-deleted top-level fields `enforcement`, `beadsWorkflow`, or `mcp` THEN those fields SHALL be stripped and the file rewritten, with a log message naming each removed key.
6. The migration code SHALL be idempotent — re-running update against an already-migrated project SHALL be a no-op (no log noise except the "no changes" path).

## Files Likely Touched

- src/update.ts — wire a new `applyDeleteriousMigrations(cwd, log, warn)` step before `installAll`
- src/install.ts — extract the migration helper; it consumes `existsSync`, `readdirSync`, `rmSync`, `readFileSync`, `writeFileSync`, plus the existing `trimBeadsIntegrationBlock` export
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — new test suite asserting orphan removal, BEADS INTEGRATION trim, legacy-field stripping, idempotency

## Verification Commands

- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope

- Auto-deleting user-authored plans / research markdown files. We only warn.
- Migrating individual issue or memory data inside `.beads/` (bd handles that with its own tooling).
close_reason: All 6 AC met. Added applyDeleteriousMigrations(cwd) to src/install.ts: removes legacy .claude/commands/spec.md and .claude/specs/TEMPLATE.md; deletes empty .claude/specs/ but warns when user-authored markdown remains; warns about non-empty .claude/plans/ and docs/research/ without auto-deleting; runs trimBeadsIntegrationBlock on AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md; strips enforcement/beadsWorkflow/mcp fields from .dev.config.json. Idempotent. Wired into src/update.ts after pruneRetiredHookFiles, before installAll. 8 new tests in install.test.ts cover each branch + idempotency + clean-slate. Verified: bun test install (21 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: Remove legacy /spec /plan /research slash commands entirely
description:
## User story

As the user, I do not want the package to ever ship slash commands that write spec/plan/research markdown to disk, regardless of whether beads is enabled. bd is the source of truth; if bd is not enabled, those concepts have no canonical home in this template, and the legacy commands should not exist.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. WHEN `generateCommands(config)` is called for any config THEN the result SHALL NOT contain `spec.md`, `plan.md`, or `research.md` unless beads is enabled (in which case `plan.md` / `research.md` are the bd-native versions added in B5; `spec.md` is never generated).
2. WHEN `generateCommands(config)` is called with `tools = []` THEN the result SHALL NOT contain `spec.md`, `plan.md`, or `research.md` at all.
3. The functions `specCommand()`, the legacy `planCommand()` (writing to .claude/plans/), and the legacy `researchCommand()` (writing to docs/research/) SHALL be deleted from `src/generate/commands.ts`. The bd-native `bdPlanCommand()` and `bdResearchCommand()` retain those filenames when beads is enabled.
4. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs against a project with `tools = []` AND a stale `.claude/commands/spec.md` exists THEN the file SHALL be left in place (manual cleanup OK; no purge).

## Files Likely Touched

- src/generate/commands.ts — delete `specCommand` and the legacy plain `planCommand`/`researchCommand`; rename the bd-native `bdPlanCommand`/`bdResearchCommand` back to `planCommand`/`researchCommand` since they are now the only versions
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts — update "keeps legacy /spec /plan /research" test to assert NONE of them generate when beads is absent
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — remove assertions that referenced legacy commands

## Verification Commands

- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts src/__tests__/install.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit
- ! grep -E "specCommand\(\)|legacy" src/generate/commands.ts

## Out of Scope

- Migration of downstream `.claude/commands/spec.md` files (manual cleanup).
- Touching `/explore`, `/verify`, `/commit`, `/ready` — these are workflow-agnostic.
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Deleted specCommand, legacy planCommand (writing to .claude/plans/), and legacy researchCommand (writing to docs/research/) from src/generate/commands.ts. Renamed bdPlanCommand/bdResearchCommand/bdDecisionCommand to canonical planCommand/researchCommand/decisionCommand since they are now the only versions. Removed the !tools.includes('beads') branch in generateCommands — when beads is absent, none of /spec /plan /research generate. Updated test 'keeps legacy /spec /plan /research when beads is not in tools' to assert all three are omitted. Verified: bun test smoke+install (82 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: Remove unwanted configurability flags from enforcement / beadsWorkflow / mcp
description:
## User story

As the user of this harness, I want hardening protections to just *be on*, so that I do not have to remember to enable them and consumers do not get a watered-down install by accident.

## Acceptance Criteria

1. WHEN `.dev.config.json` is loaded THEN `DevConfig` SHALL NOT contain an `enforcement` block, a `beadsWorkflow` block, or an `mcp` block.
2. WHEN `generateClaudeSettings(config)` runs AND `config.targets` includes `'claude'` THEN the generated `settings.json` SHALL unconditionally include: `baseline-pin.sh` (SessionStart), `baseline-compare.sh` (Stop), `docs-first.sh` (PreToolUse Read|Glob), `audit-log.sh` (PreToolUse no-matcher), `citation-check.sh` (Stop), `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` on PreToolUse + PostToolUse + Stop.
3. WHEN `tools` includes `'beads'` THEN generated settings SHALL unconditionally include `require-claim.sh` (PreToolUse Write|Edit) AND register a `context7` mcpServer entry.
4. WHEN `tools` includes `'beads'` AND `workflow === 'bd'` THEN `installAll` SHALL unconditionally run `seedConstitutionDecisions` (already idempotent).
5. WHEN `installAll` runs with any `claude` target THEN `.gitignore` SHALL contain `.claude/audit.jsonl`.
6. The previously-shipped fields SHALL be deleted from `src/config.ts`: `EnforcementConfig`, `BeadsWorkflowConfig`, `McpConfig`, `IssueTemplateFormat`, plus `enforcement`, `beadsWorkflow`, `mcp` on `DevConfig`.

## Files Likely Touched

- src/config.ts — delete the four interfaces and three fields
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — drop all `if (X)` gates around hook registration
- src/install.ts — drop the gate around `seedConstitutionDecisions` call (still gated on workflow='bd' + tools includes beads); always run `appendToGitignore('.claude/audit.jsonl')` for claude target
- src/__tests__/config.test.ts — drop default-off tests and partial-block tests; add "fields are not on DevConfig"
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts — replace "omits when X is unset" / "registers when X is true" pairs with single "always registers" assertions
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — no change unless install.test asserted on flag behavior

## Verification Commands

- bun test src/__tests__/config.test.ts src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit
- ! grep -rE "config\.enforcement|config\.beadsWorkflow|config\.mcp" src/

## Out of Scope

- Removing the workflow type narrowing from D3 ('bd' | 'none' stays — that is *type* simplification, not a configurability knob).
- Removing the audit log entirely. The hook stays; only the flag goes.
close_reason: All 6 AC met. Stripped EnforcementConfig, BeadsWorkflowConfig, McpConfig, IssueTemplateFormat interfaces and the enforcement / beadsWorkflow / mcp fields from DevConfig (src/config.ts). All hardening hooks now register unconditionally in src/generate/claude-settings.ts: baseline-pin / baseline-compare / docs-first / audit-log / citation-check + ai-antipattern-guard on Pre+Post+Stop. require-claim.sh and context7 mcpServer auto-register when tools includes 'beads'. installAll runs appendToGitignore('.claude/audit.jsonl') unconditionally for any claude target. Tests rewritten as 'always registers X' instead of conditional pairs (smoke +8 tests, config simplified). Verification (AC #6 grep): zero refs to config.enforcement / config.beadsWorkflow / config.mcp remain. All 147 tests pass; tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Harness hardening: bd-native workflow + verification depth
description:
## User story

As a developer using @oisincoveney/dev to scaffold AI agent harnesses, I want generated projects to enforce verification, prevent hallucinations, prevent hooks evasion, and make beads the single source of truth for specs/plans/research/decisions, so that downstream consumers stop fighting confident-wrong agents and have a workflow that produces verified, ticket-bound, TDD-driven changes.

## Success Criteria

1. WHEN a generated project starts a Claude Code session AND `enforcement.baselinePin` is true THE SYSTEM SHALL pin the baseline failing-test set and refuse Stop if any new test fails relative to baseline.
2. WHEN a generated project's agent reads a path inside `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `.next/`, `target/`, or `generated/` without `ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1` AND `enforcement.docsFirst` is true THE SYSTEM SHALL block the read with a message directing the agent to WebFetch official docs first.
3. WHEN a generated project has `tools=["beads"]` AND `bd` is on PATH AND `.beads/` does not exist THE SYSTEM SHALL run `bd init && bd hooks install` automatically during `oisin-dev init`.
4. WHEN the user invokes `/epic`, `/work-next`, `/verify-spec`, `/discover`, `/plan`, `/research`, `/decision`, or `/bd-hygiene` THE SYSTEM SHALL operate exclusively against `bd`; no markdown spec/plan/research artifacts SHALL be created on disk.
5. WHEN the agent attempts Edit/Write to a source file without an `in_progress` claimed bd issue AND `beadsWorkflow.requireClaim` is true THE SYSTEM SHALL block the edit.
6. WHEN the agent claims completion without having executed the configured test command this session THE SYSTEM SHALL block the Stop event (existing behavior — verify it still passes after changes).
7. WHEN `/verify-spec <id>` runs THE SYSTEM SHALL spawn a fresh-context subagent that re-reads `bd show <id>`, runs each Verification Command, and returns PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL with per-criterion evidence; FAIL/PARTIAL SHALL block `bd close`.
8. WHEN `/discover <description>` is invoked during work THE SYSTEM SHALL create a child bd issue with `--deps=discovered-from:<current>` rather than allowing silent scope expansion.
9. IF `mcp.context7` is enabled in `.dev.config.json` THEN THE SYSTEM SHALL register a `context7` entry under `mcpServers` in the generated `.claude/settings.json`.
10. WHEN the package's own test suite runs (`bun test`) THE SYSTEM SHALL pass with new tests covering: baseline-pin, docs-first, require-claim, `/epic` epic+children creation, `/verify-spec` PASS/FAIL behavior, multi-event hook coverage, and EARS template compliance.
11. WHEN `oisin-dev init` completes for a project with `workflow="bd"` THEN `bd config validation.on-create` SHALL be set to `warn` and (in phase D) flipped to `error` once all built-in templates are EARS-compliant.
12. WHEN this epic closes THE `workflow` config field SHALL accept only `'bd'` or `'none'`; the legacy `'idd'` and `'gsd'` values SHALL be removed.

## Out of Scope

- Cursor / Codex / OpenCode equivalents of new bd-native commands (Claude Code only this round).
- Symbol-existence hooks for non-TypeScript languages (Rust/Go/Swift get no-op stubs; full coverage is a follow-up epic).
- Migration tooling for existing downstream projects with `.claude/specs/` legacy artifacts (manual one-time cleanup is acceptable).
- Phase-gated TDD subagents (red/green/refactor isolation) — deferred to a follow-up epic; this epic relies on existing `tdd-guard.sh`.

## Constitution references

- bd is the single source of truth for specs, plans, research, decisions.
- No `.claude/specs/`, `.claude/plans/`, or `docs/research/` directories generated.
- TDD enforced (red → green → refactor).
- No follow-up questions in agent output.
- Verification before completion claims.
- One ticket per session; one `in_progress` per worker.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: A4: Update flow integration with manifest + .dev-new
description:
## User story
As the update flow, I need to read the prior manifest, write .dev-new sidecars for drifted files (one warning per drift), halt loudly on lefthook drift, and remove retired files.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN update runs AND a tracked file has drifted THEN it SHALL write <path>.dev-new with the new content, leaving the user's file untouched.
2. WHEN the same drift was already reported (existing .dev-new identical to new content) THEN no warning is emitted (one-warning-per-content-change).
3. WHEN lefthook.yml has drifted THEN update SHALL halt with a diff and refuse to proceed without explicit --accept-lefthook-overwrite OR oisin-dev accept-lefthook command.
4. WHEN a file is in the prior manifest but NOT in the new shipped set THEN update SHALL remove it (with .dev-new if drifted).
5. WHEN update completes THEN the new manifest SHALL be written.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts (update path)
- src/update.ts (orchestration, accept-lefthook command)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Init flow (A3)
close_reason: All 5 AC met. update.ts wires the InstallResult: drifted files surface as .dev-new (already implemented in applyManagedFiles, AC #1, #2 inherited). Lefthook drift halts the update with a clear three-option message: re-run with --accept-lefthook-overwrite to take new, run 'oisin-dev accept-lefthook' to keep yours and update the manifest hash, or reconcile manually (AC #3). Retired files removed with .dev-new sidecar if drifted (AC #4 — already in applyManagedFiles, write empty .dev-new as the marker). New manifest written at end (AC #5 — already in applyManagedFiles). New src/accept-lefthook.ts command + cli.ts wiring for the keep-yours alternative path. Verified: 120 tests pass, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: B4: Verifier rule + integration with code-review skill selection
description:
## User story
As an agent finishing ticket work, I need a behavioral rule that codifies the verifier subagent contract — load code-review skill, plus skill-aware selection by diff content, and the self-repeating loop semantics (verifier files new tickets, main agent claims them).

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN templates/rules/verifier-loop.md is shipped THEN it SHALL describe: invoke fresh-context subagent before bd close, load code-review skill always, load architecture / testing-strategy / security-review / accessibility / performance / tech-debt skills based on diff content, return PASS / PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS / PARTIAL / FAIL.
2. The rule SHALL state: for issues outside the original AC, file new bd issues via bd create --type=task --deps=discovered-from:<current>. Do not silently fix inline.
3. The rule SHALL describe the loop: PASS or PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS -> bd close + auto-claim next ready (which may be the just-filed followups). PARTIAL/FAIL -> fix in-place in the current ticket, do not close, re-run.
4. The rule SHALL be loaded by generateRules() into .claude/rules/verifier-loop.md.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/rules/verifier-loop.md (new)
- src/skills.ts
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- The verifier hook implementation (D1)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/rules/verifier-loop.md describes: when to invoke (always before bd close), how to invoke (Agent subagent_type=general-purpose with self-contained prompt), skill loading protocol (code-review + tech-debt always; architecture/testing-strategy/security-review/accessibility/performance based on diff content), structured output format with per-criterion / verification commands / scope check / new tickets sections, four aggregate results (PASS / PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS / PARTIAL / FAIL), filing format for discovered-from issues, main-agent behavior per result with the self-repeating loop on PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS, loop termination rule, and hard rules (no self-verification, no silent inline fixes, no bd close until PASS or PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS). generateRules() loads it into .claude/rules/verifier-loop.md when tools includes beads. New smoke test. Verified: bun test smoke (72 pass), tsc clean.

title: D1: Verifier subagent skill (templates/skills/spec-verifier/)
description:
## User story
As the agent finishing ticket work, I want a vendored spec-verifier skill the main agent invokes that runs the contract from B4: load code-review + skill-aware extras, check ACs, file new tickets for issues outside scope, return structured PASS/PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS/PARTIAL/FAIL.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN templates/skills/spec-verifier/SKILL.md is shipped THEN it SHALL be a fresh-context subagent prompt invoking code-review skill plus diff-aware extras.
2. The skill prompt SHALL instruct: re-read bd show <id>, list each EARS criterion, run each Verification Command, check scope vs Files Likely Touched, file new bd tickets for issues outside the original AC.
3. The skill output format SHALL be structured: { result: 'PASS'|'PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS'|'PARTIAL'|'FAIL', perCriterion: [{ criterion, status, evidence }], newTickets: string[], scopeViolations: string[] }.
4. The skill SHALL be installed by installAll into .claude/skills/spec-verifier/ when targets includes claude AND tools includes beads.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/skills/spec-verifier/SKILL.md (new)
- src/skills.ts (register)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- The actual code-review skill (vendored separately or expected to be installed via SUPERPOWER_SKILLS)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/skills/spec-verifier/SKILL.md is a fresh-context subagent prompt: re-read bd show <id>, capture diff, load code-review + tech-debt always plus diff-aware extras (architecture / testing-strategy / security-review / accessibility / performance), per-criterion review with file:line evidence, run each Verification Command and capture exit codes, scope check against Files Likely Touched, file new bd tickets via bd create --type=task --deps discovered-from for issues outside original AC (no silent inline fixes), return structured PASS / PASS-WITH-FOLLOWUPS / PARTIAL / FAIL output. Forbidden actions: bd close, bd update, source modifications, context reuse from orchestrator. installOwnedSkills now gates spec-verifier (and to-bd-issues) on tools.includes('beads') via BD_ONLY_SKILLS set so non-bd consumers do not get bd-specific skills. New install.test.ts assertion. Verified: bun test install (21 pass), tsc clean.

title: B1: Vendor mattpocock/skills/grill-me with MIT attribution
description:
## User story
As the package, I want to ship Matt Pocock's grill-me skill verbatim so consumers can opt into it via the planning menu. MIT license + attribution preserved.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN templates/skills/grill-me/ is shipped THEN it SHALL contain the verbatim SKILL.md from mattpocock/skills@<commit-sha>.
2. WHEN the skill is shipped THEN it SHALL include a LICENSE file with Matt Pocock's MIT copyright and the standard MIT permission notice.
3. WHEN the skill is shipped THEN the SKILL.md SHALL include a one-line attribution comment at top noting the upstream source URL.
4. The skill SHALL be installed by installAll into .claude/skills/grill-me/ when targets includes claude.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/skills/grill-me/SKILL.md (new, vendored)
- templates/skills/grill-me/LICENSE (new, MIT)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- to-bd-issues fork (B2)
- Planning UX rule (B3)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. templates/skills/grill-me/SKILL.md vendored verbatim from mattpocock/skills/grill-me with an HTML-comment attribution at top noting upstream URL. templates/skills/grill-me/LICENSE preserves Matt Pocock's MIT copyright + standard MIT permission notice + source URL. installOwnedSkills already iterates templates/skills/ recursively, so no install.ts changes needed — the new directory copies through unchanged. New install.test.ts assertions verify both files land at .claude/skills/grill-me/ with expected content. Verified: bun test install (21 pass).

title: A2: Drift detection helpers (sha256 + super-drift)
description:
## User story
As the install/update flow, I need helpers that compute file hashes, classify drift severity (none / mild / super), and decide which mitigation applies.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN hashFile(path) is called THEN it SHALL return the sha256 hex digest of the file contents (or null if file missing).
2. WHEN classifyDrift(currentContent, expectedContent) is called THEN it SHALL return 'none' | 'mild' | 'super' based on: none = bytes match; super = added line count > 20 OR line-count delta > 25%; mild = otherwise.
3. WHEN currentContent or expectedContent is empty/missing THEN classifyDrift SHALL not throw; it SHALL return 'super' for one-side-empty.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/manifest.ts (extend)
- src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- File I/O orchestration (A3, A4)
close_reason: All 3 AC met. Added hashFile(path) returning sha256 hex digest (or null when missing) and classifyDrift(current, expected) returning 'none'|'mild'|'super'. Super-drift triggers on absolute added-line count > 20 OR line-count-delta ratio > 25%. One-side-empty returns 'super'. Both-empty returns 'none'. 10 new tests cover all branches. Verified: bun test (19 pass total in manifest.test.ts), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: E2: Bump 0.8.0 + seed manifest from known 0.7.x shipped files
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer upgrading from 0.7.x to 0.8.0, I want the first oisin-dev update to seed the manifest from a hardcoded list of files we know we shipped in 0.7.x so the manifest takes over cleanly.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN package.json version is 0.8.0 AND .claude/.dev-manifest.json does NOT exist AND a .claude/ install is detected (e.g., .claude/hooks/banned-words-guard.sh exists) THEN installAll SHALL seed the manifest from a hardcoded list of files known to have shipped in 0.7.x, hashing each file's CURRENT content as the recorded sha256.
2. WHEN the manifest is seeded THEN no .user-backup or .dev-new files SHALL be created (the seed treats current content as authoritative).
3. The hardcoded list SHALL be exported from a single source (src/manifest.ts) so future versions can reference 'what 0.7.x shipped' if needed.
4. WHEN the seed completes AND a fresh installAll runs as part of the same update THEN normal manifest-driven flow takes over.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts (seed step)
- src/manifest.ts (KNOWN_07X_FILES)
- package.json (bump to 0.8.0)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Migration from <0.7.0 (out of support)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. (1) package.json bumped from 0.7.0 to 0.8.0. (2) New seedManifestFromKnownFiles(cwd, version) export in src/manifest.ts: when no manifest exists AND any KNOWN_07X_FILES path is on disk, seeds the manifest using the file's CURRENT content as the authoritative sha256. No .user-backup or .dev-new during seed (current content is canonical). (3) KNOWN_07X_FILES exported as ReadonlyArray<string> covering all 21 hooks shipped in 0.7.x. (4) installAll calls seedManifestFromKnownFiles BEFORE applyManagedFiles when claude target is selected — idempotent (no-op when manifest already exists). 6 new manifest tests cover seed-from-disk, hash authority, idempotency, missing-file skip, no-files-on-disk path, and KNOWN_07X_FILES shape. Verified: 36 manifest + 99 smoke/install + 13 e2e = 148 tests, tsc --noEmit clean.

title: A1: Manifest schema + reader/writer
description:
## User story
As the package, I need a typed read/write API for the .claude/.dev-manifest.json so all downstream code paths share one source of truth for file ownership.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN readManifest(cwd) is called AND .claude/.dev-manifest.json exists THEN it SHALL return { version: string, files: Record<relPath, { sha256: string }> }.
2. WHEN the file is missing THEN readManifest SHALL return null.
3. WHEN writeManifest(cwd, manifest) is called THEN it SHALL serialize the manifest as JSON with 2-space indent and trailing newline.
4. WHEN parsing fails (malformed JSON, missing required fields) THEN readManifest SHALL throw with a precise error.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/manifest.ts (new)
- src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts (new)

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope
- Drift detection (A2)
- Init/update wiring (A3, A4)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New src/manifest.ts exports Manifest interface, readManifest(cwd), writeManifest(cwd, m). readManifest returns null when file missing, throws with precise messages on malformed JSON / missing version / missing files / entry missing sha256. writeManifest creates parent .claude/ dir, serializes with 2-space indent + trailing newline. 9 tests in src/__tests__/manifest.test.ts cover round-trip, missing-file, JSON formatting, parent-dir creation, and all four error paths. Verified: bun test (9 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: A3: Init flow integration with manifest + .user-backup
description:
## User story
As the init flow, I need to read the prior manifest, hash-check each tracked file, replace clean files silently, back up mildly-drifted files, prompt interactively on super-drift, halt on lefthook drift, and emit an end-of-init summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN init runs AND prior manifest exists THEN it SHALL hash-check every file in the prior manifest before writing.
2. WHEN a file's hash matches its prior manifest hash THEN init SHALL replace silently.
3. WHEN a file is mildly drifted THEN init SHALL move it to <path>.user-backup BEFORE writing fresh.
4. WHEN a file is super-drifted THEN init SHALL prompt the user interactively (keep / take / show diff / abort) BEFORE proceeding.
5. WHEN lefthook.yml is drifted (any severity) THEN init SHALL halt with a diff and require explicit acknowledgment to proceed.
6. WHEN a file is in the prior manifest but NOT in the new shipped set THEN init SHALL remove it (with .user-backup if drifted).
7. WHEN init completes THEN it SHALL print an end-of-init summary listing every .user-backup created (omit summary if zero).
8. The new manifest SHALL be written reflecting the current shipped set.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts (init path)
- src/init.ts (orchestration)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope
- Update flow (A4)
- Retirement of applyDeleteriousMigrations (E1)
close_reason: All 8 AC met. Added applyManagedFiles(cwd, options) to src/manifest.ts that handles fresh writes, clean replace, mild drift (.user-backup on init), super drift (deferred for prompt on init / .dev-new on update), lefthook drift (halt with details), and removal of retired files (with .user-backup if drifted). Wired into installAll for .claude/hooks/ as the proof-of-pattern. installAll now returns InstallResult with manifest results bubbled up. init.ts halts on lefthook drift, prompts interactively per super-drifted file (keep/take/abort) using @clack/prompts p.select, and logs end-of-init summary (backups, removed). 12 new applyManagedFiles tests + super-drift detail retention. Verified: 120 tests pass across manifest+install+smoke; tsc --noEmit clean. Note: only .claude/hooks/ migrated to manifest in this ticket; skills, commands, rules, lint configs, settings.json migrate in follow-up tickets filed as discovered-from.

title: B3: Planning UX rule (templates/rules/planning-ux.md)
description:
## User story
As an agent in a downstream project, I need a behavioral rule that codifies the menu-driven planning UX: surface 2-4 feature-domain options + Grill me + standard process overrides after every multi-file intent.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN templates/rules/planning-ux.md is shipped THEN it SHALL describe: when to surface a menu (multi-file or multi-concern intent without an in_progress claim), what options to include (feature-domain options about the actual problem; never about ticket structure), the standard process overrides (Grill me, Chat about it, Just do it, Defer).
2. The rule SHALL state when to use AskUserQuestion (small mutex options that benefit from chip UI) vs markdown menu (longer descriptions, voice context).
3. The rule SHALL forbid agent-side unilateral filing of epics; the agent drafts, the user approves.
4. The rule SHALL be loaded by generateRules() into .claude/rules/planning-ux.md.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/rules/planning-ux.md (new)
- src/skills.ts (add to RULE_SKILLS or always-on rules)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Mechanical enforcement (none for grill-me; require-swarm.sh in C2 for edits)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/rules/planning-ux.md describes: when to surface a menu (multi-file/multi-concern intent without an in_progress claim), feature-domain options on top + standard process overrides on bottom (Grill me, Chat, Just do it, Defer), AskUserQuestion vs markdown decision criteria, hard rules (never unilateral epic, never invoke grill-me unilaterally, never restructure user intent, no follow-up questions). generateRules() loads it from templates/rules/planning-ux.md when tools includes beads. 2 new smoke tests. Verified: bun test smoke (71 pass), tsc --noEmit clean.

title: Resolve BEADS INTEGRATION block conflict in AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md
description:
## User story
As a project maintainer, I want the BEADS INTEGRATION block injected by `bd hooks install` to NOT contradict project rules (specifically "Stop before pushing. The user pushes when they're ready"), so that agents do not get conflicting instructions.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md are read by an AI agent THEN they SHALL NOT contain "MUST push to remote" or "Work is NOT complete until git push succeeds" or similar mandatory-push directives.
2. The bd-related Quick Reference (`bd ready`, `bd show`, `bd update --claim`, `bd close`) SHALL be retained.
3. The Rules block stating "Use bd for ALL task tracking" SHALL be retained.
4. The package's own `installAll` SHALL NOT regenerate this conflicting block on `oisin-dev update` (covered partly by B8 workflow rule rewrite).

## Files Likely Touched
- AGENTS.md — trim BEADS INTEGRATION block to retain Quick Reference + Rules, remove Session Completion / mandatory-push section
- CLAUDE.md — same treatment
- (downstream, in B1) src/install.ts SHALL skip or post-process `bd hooks install` output to avoid re-injecting conflict

## Verification Commands
- grep -E '(MUST push|NEVER stop before pushing|Work is NOT complete until)' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md && exit 1 || exit 0

## Out of Scope
- Upstream fix in bd itself (bd's hooks install behavior).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Trimmed AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md to remove 'Session Completion' / 'MUST push to remote' / 'Work is NOT complete until git push succeeds' content. Quick Reference and Rules retained. Added trimBeadsIntegrationBlock(path) helper in src/install.ts; configureBeadsAfterInit calls it on AGENTS.md and CLAUDE.md after 'bd hooks install' so future installs do not re-introduce the conflict. 3 new e2e-install tests cover: trim removes Session Completion, no-op when block missing, no-op when section already absent. AC4 verification: grep -E '(MUST push|NEVER stop before pushing|Work is NOT complete until)' AGENTS.md CLAUDE.md returns nothing. Verified: bun test (11 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: C1: docs-first.sh PreToolUse hook for Read/Glob
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want the agent blocked from reading buried dependency files when official docs would be authoritative, so that hallucinated APIs are not "verified" by reading stale node_modules/dist content.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.docsFirst === true` THEN `docs-first.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` for matchers `Read|Glob`.
2. WHEN a Read/Glob tool call targets a path matching `^(node_modules|dist|build|\.next|target|generated|out)/` AND env `ALLOW_DEPS_READ` is unset or empty THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with message: "Use WebFetch on official docs first. Set `ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1` in env to override for this turn."
3. WHEN env `ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1` THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.
4. WHEN the path does not match any blocked prefix THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/docs-first.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — wire on PreToolUse Read|Glob gated by `enforcement.docsFirst`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Soft-warn mode (binary on/off only).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added templates/hooks/docs-first.sh PreToolUse Read|Glob hook. Blocks reads where path matches node_modules / dist / build / .next / target / generated / out (with or without leading dirs). Override per-turn via env ALLOW_DEPS_READ=1. Wired in claude-settings.ts gated by enforcement.docsFirst. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (72 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: B8: Rewrite workflow rule + remove .claude/specs creation
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want generated projects to contain no on-disk spec/plan/research directories, and I want the workflow rule to describe the bd-native loop, so that the documented workflow matches the implemented workflow.

3. WHEN `oisin-dev update` runs against an existing project with a stale `.claude/specs/TEMPLATE.md` THEN the file SHALL be left in place (manual cleanup) but the workflow rule SHALL be regenerated.

title: B7: require-claim.sh PreToolUse hook
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want Edit/Write to source files to be blocked unless a bd issue is currently `in_progress`, so that the agent cannot drift outside the claimed ticket's scope.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.beadsWorkflow.requireClaim === true` THEN `require-claim.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` for matchers `Write|Edit`.
2. WHEN a Write/Edit tool call targets a path matching `^src/`, `^lib/`, `^app/`, or any `.ts|.tsx|.js|.rs|.go|.swift` file outside `.claude/`, `.beads/`, `tests/`, `__tests__/`, `*.test.*`, `*.spec.*`, `node_modules/`, `dist/`, `build/`, `target/`, README/docs, AND no bd issue is `in_progress` THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with message "No claimed bd issue. Run /work-next or `bd update <id> --claim` first."
3. WHEN any bd issue is `in_progress` THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.
4. IF `bd` is not on PATH THEN the hook SHALL fail-open (exit 0).

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/require-claim.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — register the hook gated by `beadsWorkflow.requireClaim`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert hook is registered when flag is set

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Enforcing that edits stay within the claimed ticket's `Files Likely Touched` list (would require parsing the issue body; deferred).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/hooks/require-claim.sh PreToolUse hook for Write|Edit; exits 2 when source-file edit attempted with no in_progress bd issue, exits 0 for tests/docs/.claude/.beads/dependency dirs. Fail-open if bd not on PATH. claude-settings.ts gated by beadsWorkflow.requireClaim. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover both branches. Verified: bun test smoke (58 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: B4: /verify-spec slash command — fresh-context subagent verification
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `/verify-spec <id>` to spawn a fresh-context subagent that re-reads the bd issue and checks each acceptance criterion against the actual code, so that the main agent cannot self-certify completion.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `/verify-spec <id>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL spawn an Agent with `subagent_type=general-purpose` and a self-contained prompt that includes the issue ID and instructions to re-read it via `bd show <id>`.
2. The subagent prompt SHALL instruct: read each EARS acceptance criterion, identify the relevant code paths, run each Verification Command from the issue, and return a structured PASS / FAIL / PARTIAL result with per-criterion evidence.
3. WHEN the subagent returns FAIL or PARTIAL THEN the main agent SHALL NOT call `bd close` and SHALL post the verifier output as a `bd note <id>` for audit trail.
4. WHEN the subagent returns PASS THEN the main agent MAY call `bd close <id> --reason "verified" --suggest-next`.
5. WHEN `$ARGUMENTS` is empty THEN the command SHALL halt and request an issue ID.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `verifySpecCommand()`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert generated command spec matches contract

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Implementing a custom subagent type (uses general-purpose).
- Mechanically blocking `bd close` from the main agent (relies on slash-command discipline + the verifier's note as evidence).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added /verify-spec slash command. Spawns subagent_type=general-purpose with self-contained prompt instructing the verifier to: bd show <id>, identify code paths per EARS criterion (cite file:line), run each Verification Command (capture exit codes), check scope vs Files Likely Touched, output structured PASS/FAIL/PARTIAL with per-criterion evidence. Result is appended as bd note for audit trail. PASS allows main agent to bd close; FAIL/PARTIAL blocks close. Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. New test in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (70 pass).

title: B3: /work-next slash command
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `/work-next` to pick the highest-priority unblocked bd issue, claim it, and read me back the acceptance criteria, so that I never start coding without an in_progress claim.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN user invokes `/work-next` THEN the command SHALL run `bd ready --json` and select the highest-priority unblocked issue.
2. WHEN no unblocked issues exist THEN the command SHALL print the reason (`bd ready --explain`) and halt without claiming.
3. WHEN an issue is selected THEN the command SHALL run `bd show <id>` and `bd update <id> --claim` (transitioning to `in_progress`).
4. WHEN claim succeeds THEN the command SHALL echo back the EARS Acceptance Criteria and Verification Commands from the issue body verbatim, and halt before any source edit.
5. IF the issue body is missing Acceptance Criteria or Verification Commands sections THEN the command SHALL warn and refuse to claim until the issue is updated (`bd update --acceptance ...`).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `workNextCommand()`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- The TDD enforcement loop itself (relies on existing tdd-guard.sh).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added /work-next slash command (workNextCommand in src/generate/commands.ts). Body instructs Claude to: bd ready --json, halt with bd ready --explain when empty, bd show, validate Acceptance Criteria + Verification Commands sections (refuse claim if missing), validate no [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers, bd update <id> --status in_progress, echo AC/VC/Files Likely Touched/Out of Scope verbatim, halt before edits. Pairs with require-claim.sh hook (B7). Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (64 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean prior).

title: B2: /epic slash command — bd-native epic + children creation
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `/epic <slug>` to draft an EARS-format spec body inline and create a bd epic with child tasks directly, so that no spec markdown file is ever written to disk.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN user invokes `/epic <slug>` THEN the command SHALL draft an EARS-format epic body and call `bd create --type=epic --body-file=- --silent` to create the epic and capture its ID.
2. WHEN the epic is created THEN the command SHALL prompt the user to confirm the child-task breakdown, then call `bd create --type=task --parent=<epic-id>` for each child.
3. WHEN `$ARGUMENTS` is empty THEN the command SHALL halt and request a kebab-case slug.
4. The command SHALL NOT write any markdown file outside of bd's database.
5. The command SHALL NOT call `bd update --claim` — claiming is the user's explicit action via `/work-next`.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `epicCommand()`, register in `generateCommands`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert `.claude/commands/epic.md` is generated with bd-native body, no spec template

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Removing the legacy `/spec` command (B6 handles that).
- The TEMPLATE.md cleanup (B8 handles that).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added /epic slash command via epicCommand() in src/generate/commands.ts. Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. Body instructs Claude to: draft EARS-format epic with [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] markers, halt if markers unresolved, create via 'bd create --type=epic --body-file=- --silent', propose child-task breakdown, batch-create children with --parent, add bd dep links, run bd lint. Explicitly forbids 'bd update --claim' (left to /work-next) and forbids writing markdown files outside bd. title: B1: Auto-run bd init and bd hooks install in installAll
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer running `oisin-dev init` with beads selected, I want bd to be initialized and its git-hook integration installed automatically, so that I do not have to remember to run `bd init` separately.

## Acceptance Criteria

4. WHEN `options.skipSideEffects` is true (test mode) THEN none of the bd commands SHALL run.

## Files Likely Touched

## Out of Scope

Sets validation.on-create=warn and runs bd hooks install. Idempotent. Skipped when bd is not on PATH or .beads/ does not exist. title: A3: Wire baseline hooks into claude-settings generator
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want `enforcement.baselinePin: true` to register the baseline hooks automatically in `.claude/settings.json`, so that pinning is opt-in via config, not manual.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.baselinePin === true` THEN generated `.claude/settings.json` SHALL include `baseline-pin.sh` on `SessionStart` (after `context-bootstrap.sh`).
2. WHEN `config.enforcement.baselinePin === true` THEN generated settings SHALL include `baseline-compare.sh` on `Stop` (before `banned-words-guard.sh`).
3. WHEN `config.enforcement.baselinePin` is unset or false THEN neither hook SHALL appear in generated settings.
4. The ordering on Stop SHALL be: `pre-stop-verification.sh` → `baseline-compare.sh` → `banned-words-guard.sh`.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands

- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope
- Implementation of the hooks themselves (A2).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Modified src/generate/claude-settings.ts: when config.enforcement.baselinePin is true, baseline-pin.sh registers on SessionStart after context-bootstrap.sh, and baseline-compare.sh registers on Stop between pre-stop-verification.sh and banned-words-guard.sh. When unset/false, neither hook appears. 4 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover all branches and ordering. Verified: bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts (51 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: A2: Implement baseline-pin.sh and baseline-compare.sh hooks
description:
## User story
As a developer running Claude Code in a generated project, I want failing tests on `main` to be pinned at session start so that the agent cannot dismiss a failure as "pre-existing" without proof, and any regression introduced this session blocks Stop.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN SessionStart fires AND the working tree is clean THE `baseline-pin.sh` SHALL checkout the merge-base of the current branch and `main`, run `commands.test --reporter=json` (or framework equivalent), capture the failing-test set into `.claude/baseline-failures.json`, and restore the working tree.
2. IF the working tree is dirty (uncommitted changes) THEN `baseline-pin.sh` SHALL skip baseline capture and write `.claude/baseline-failures.json` with `{"skipped": true, "reason": "dirty checkout"}`.
3. WHEN Stop fires AND `.claude/baseline-failures.json` exists AND `skipped` is false THE `baseline-compare.sh` SHALL run the test command, compute the failing-test set, and exit 2 if any test fails now that did not fail in baseline; the exit message SHALL list the regression delta.
4. IF baseline JSON is missing or `skipped: true` THEN `baseline-compare.sh` SHALL exit 0 (fail-open).
5. The hooks SHALL fail-open on errors (missing test command, jq parse failure, git failure) — never block Stop on infrastructure failure.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/baseline-pin.sh — new
- templates/hooks/baseline-compare.sh — new
- src/__tests__/baseline-pin.test.ts — new (fixture repo with baseline-failing test, assert regression detection)

## Verification Commands

Pin captures failing-test set on merge-base of current branch and main; compare exits 2 with regression delta when new failures appear. Fail-open on dirty checkout, missing config, missing baseline, missing test cmd, parse errors, git failures. Integration test src/__tests__/baseline-pin.test.ts (10 tests) covers all branches. Fixed BSD sed regex (used [[:space:]] instead of \\s). Verified: bun test (config/baseline/banned-words/install/prune/run-command/smoke/e2e — all green individually).

title: A1: Add enforcement and beadsWorkflow schema fields to DevConfig
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want fine-grained on/off switches in `.dev.config.json` for each new enforcement and workflow capability, so that I can adopt them incrementally without flipping everything at once.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `.dev.config.json` contains `enforcement.{baselinePin,docsFirst,symbolCheck,auditLog,multiEvent}` (each boolean, optional) THEN `readConfig` SHALL parse them without error.
2. WHEN `.dev.config.json` contains `beadsWorkflow.{epicTicketLoop,issueTemplates,requireClaim}` THEN `readConfig` SHALL parse them; `issueTemplates` accepts `'ears' | 'gherkin' | 'checklist'` only.
3. WHEN `.dev.config.json` contains `mcp.context7` (boolean) THEN `readConfig` SHALL parse it.
4. WHEN `writeConfig` is called with a config containing the new fields THEN the resulting JSON SHALL round-trip identically.
5. WHEN any new field is omitted THEN downstream code SHALL treat it as `false` / undefined (no breakage).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/config.ts — add nested optional interfaces
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — add round-trip test cases

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts
- tsc --noEmit

## Out of Scope
- Wiring the new flags into generators (separate tickets handle each).
- Migration of existing `.dev.config.json` files (backward compatible — missing fields read as undefined).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added EnforcementConfig, BeadsWorkflowConfig, McpConfig optional interfaces to DevConfig in src/config.ts. New test file src/__tests__/config.test.ts covers round-trip with all flags, partial blocks, omitted fields, and each issueTemplates literal. Verification: bun test (87 pass, 0 fail), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: Migrate skills + commands + rules + settings to manifest tracking
description:
## User story
A3 wired manifest-tracking for `.claude/hooks/` only. The remaining categories (skills, commands, rules, settings.json, codex hooks, opencode plugin, cursor rules, lefthook.yml, lint configs, tool configs) still use scattered writeFileSync calls and are NOT in the manifest. Migrate them so the manifest is the single source of truth for everything we ship.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN installAll runs THEN every shipped file SHALL flow through applyManagedFiles, not direct writeFileSync.
2. The single applyManagedFiles call SHALL receive a Map of all files we want to ship in this run.
3. lefthook.yml drift SHALL halt the install (already wired by A3 — needs the file to actually be in the Map).
4. settings.json deserves special handling: update mode merges (current behavior); init mode replaces. Manifest tracks the final written content.
5. The .dev-backup mechanism in installAll lines 117-138 SHALL be deleted (replaced by manifest's .user-backup / .dev-new).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts (gather all files, single applyManagedFiles call)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Skill directories tracked as units vs per-file (decide during implementation; current preference is per-file)

title: D3: discovered-from auto-creation rule + audit-log scope-drift detector
description:
## User story
As the agent, when scope expands during work (an Edit/Write targets a file outside the claimed ticket's Files Likely Touched), I want to automatically file a discovered-from child ticket so scope creep is tracked in bd, not absorbed silently.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN templates/rules/scope-discipline.md is shipped THEN it SHALL describe: discovered-from auto-creation rule, what triggers it (edit outside Files Likely Touched), the bd command to run.
2. A new audit-log post-check SHALL parse .claude/audit.jsonl after a session and report Edit/Write events whose paths were outside the claimed ticket's Files Likely Touched without a corresponding discovered-from issue having been created.
3. The post-check SHALL be runnable as bun run scripts/check-scope-drift.ts (or similar) and is documented in the rule.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/rules/scope-discipline.md (new)
- scripts/check-scope-drift.ts (new)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Pre-edit blocking on scope violations (would require parsing the issue body — defer)
close_reason: All 3 AC met. New templates/rules/scope-discipline.md describes: the rule (stop, file discovered-from ticket, stay scoped), what counts as out-of-scope, what doesn't need a discovered-from ticket (test/config/docs adjuncts), why mechanical pre-edit blocking is hard (parsing issue body is brittle), the post-check approach. Loaded by generateRules() into .claude/rules/scope-discipline.md when tools includes beads. New scripts/check-scope-drift.ts: TS bun script that parses .claude/audit.jsonl, finds Edit/Write events, queries bd for the in_progress claim's Files Likely Touched, flags edits outside that list without a corresponding discovered-from ticket. Exit 0 on clean, 1 on violations. New smoke test covers scope-discipline.md generation. Verified: bun test smoke (79 pass), tsc clean.

title: D2: Worktree-fan-out skill (templates/skills/parallel-tickets/)
description:
## User story
As the agent, I want a vendored parallel-tickets skill that orchestrates worktree-isolated sub-agents for parallel ready fronts in a swarm, capped at 3 concurrent. User opts in via menu choice.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN the user picks fan-out from a planning menu AND the active swarm has >=2 ready fronts THEN the skill SHALL spawn one subagent per ready front (capped at 3 concurrent) with isolation: worktree.
2. Each subagent SHALL be scoped to a single ticket end-to-end (claim, work, verifier, close, commit with bd-* ref).
3. WHEN any subagent fails verification THEN the skill SHALL surface the failure to the orchestrator without auto-aborting siblings.
4. The skill SHALL be installed by installAll into .claude/skills/parallel-tickets/ when targets includes claude AND tools includes beads.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/skills/parallel-tickets/SKILL.md (new)
- src/skills.ts (register)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Auto-detection of when fan-out is worth it (always user-opt-in)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/skills/parallel-tickets/SKILL.md describes orchestration: bd swarm validate first, bd ready --json --mol picks up to 3 ready fronts, spawn one Agent per ticket with isolation:worktree and a self-contained sub-agent prompt that claims+implements+verifier+closes+commits. Sub-agent cap of 3 concurrent (per research). Failure isolation: any FAIL/PARTIAL captures the message but does NOT auto-abort siblings; aggregate summary at end. Worktree cleanup: empty worktrees auto-clean, committed ones stay until manual git worktree remove. 'When NOT to fan out' section. Forbidden actions: no recursive sub-agents, no >3 concurrent, no orchestrator-side commit, no auto-merge. installOwnedSkills BD_ONLY_SKILLS gates this on tools.includes('beads'). New install.test.ts assertion. Verified: bun test install (21 pass), tsc clean.

title: E1: Retire applyDeleteriousMigrations and pruneRetiredHookFiles
description:
## User story
Once the manifest is the source of truth for ownership, the ad-hoc applyDeleteriousMigrations function and pruneRetiredHookFiles list are redundant: removed-from-manifest = removed-on-disk falls out of A3/A4 naturally.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN this ticket lands THEN src/install.ts SHALL no longer export applyDeleteriousMigrations.
2. The pruneRetiredHookFiles function and RETIRED_HOOK_FILES list in src/update.ts SHALL be removed.
3. The behavior previously covered by applyDeleteriousMigrations (legacy spec command removal, BEADS INTEGRATION trim, removed-config-field stripping) SHALL be either: covered by manifest-driven removal (legacy files), retained as standalone helpers (BEADS INTEGRATION trim runs once per init/update independently), or moved to the legacy 0.7.x-to-0.8.0 migration helper.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts (delete applyDeleteriousMigrations)
- src/update.ts (delete pruneRetiredHookFiles, RETIRED_HOOK_FILES)

- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

close_reason: All 3 AC met. applyDeleteriousMigrations and the MigrationResult interface are removed from src/install.ts. pruneRetiredHookFiles + RETIRED_HOOK_FILES list removed from src/update.ts.
