title: C1: bd-context-inject.sh UserPromptSubmit hook (additionalContext only)
description:
## User story
As the agent, I want current bd state injected into every UserPromptSubmit so I see the active claim and the top of the ready queue without the user having to ask. Never blocks the prompt.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN UserPromptSubmit fires AND bd is on PATH AND .beads/ exists THEN bd-context-inject.sh SHALL run bd state and bd ready --json and emit hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext containing the current claim summary plus the top 3 ready items.
2. WHEN bd is missing or .beads/ does not exist THEN the hook SHALL exit 0 (silent no-op).
3. The hook SHALL never set decision=block; never modify the prompt; always succeed.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/bd-context-inject.sh (new)
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts (register on UserPromptSubmit)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Blocking behavior (explicitly forbidden per user direction)
close_reason: All 3 AC met. New templates/hooks/bd-context-inject.sh: UserPromptSubmit hook that runs bd list --status in_progress --json + bd ready --json (top 3) and emits hookSpecificOutput.additionalContext with current claim summary + ready queue. Silent no-op when bd missing or .beads/ does not exist. Never sets decision=block, never modifies the prompt — additionalContext only. Wired in claude-settings.ts UserPromptSubmit when tools includes beads (alongside context-injector.sh). 2 new smoke tests cover beads-enabled and beads-disabled paths. Verified: bun test smoke (74 pass), tsc --noEmit clean.

title: C3: Lefthook commit-msg ticket-ID enforcement
description:
## User story
As the package, I want to enforce that every commit references a bd-* ticket ID either in scope (feat(bd-X.Y): ...) or in a Refs: bd-X.Y footer, except for docs/chore/style commits.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN lefthook.yml is generated AND tools includes beads THEN it SHALL include a commit-msg step that runs a script checking the message for either pattern.
2. WHEN the commit message has a bd-* reference (scope or footer) THEN the check SHALL pass.
3. WHEN the commit type is docs:, chore:, or style: AND no bd-* reference is present THEN the check SHALL pass (escape hatch for non-source changes).
4. Otherwise the check SHALL exit non-zero with a one-line explanation.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/lefthook.ts (extend commit-msg block)
- templates/hooks/commit-msg-bd-ref.sh (new helper script)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Enforcing one-commit-per-ticket (different concern; separate ticket if needed)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. lefthook commit-msg block now includes bd-ticket-ref step when tools includes beads. The check: extracts the conventional type from the subject; allows docs:/chore:/style: as escape hatches without ticket reference; otherwise requires either a (bd-XXX) scope OR a 'Refs: bd-XXX' footer; rejects with a clear message when neither is present. New beadsCommitRefStep helper in src/generate/lefthook.ts. 2 new smoke tests cover both branches. Verified: bun test smoke (78 pass), tsc --noEmit clean.

title: B2: Vendor + fork to-issues as to-bd-issues skill
description:
## User story
As the package, I want a planning-output skill that consumes a grilled conversation and writes the result as bd issues (epic + children via bd create --graph) instead of GitHub Issues.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN templates/skills/to-bd-issues/SKILL.md is shipped THEN it SHALL be derived from mattpocock/skills/to-issues with a LICENSE file preserving Matt's MIT copyright AND an attribution noting the fork.
2. WHEN the skill instructs the agent THEN it SHALL produce: an epic body in EARS format with Success Criteria, Out of Scope, and a children list with depends_on edges, then call bd create --graph plan.json.
3. WHEN the conversation has unresolved [NEEDS CLARIFICATION: ...] markers THEN the skill SHALL refuse to write to bd until they are resolved.
4. The skill SHALL be installed by installAll into .claude/skills/to-bd-issues/ when targets includes claude.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/skills/to-bd-issues/SKILL.md (new, forked)
- templates/skills/to-bd-issues/LICENSE
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- The grill-me skill itself (B1)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Forked Pocock's to-issues SKILL.md as templates/skills/to-bd-issues/SKILL.md with: HTML attribution to upstream, bd create --graph as the output target (replacing gh issue create), EARS epic + child task body templates per project conventions, [NEEDS CLARIFICATION] hard refuse-to-write gate, validation steps (bd lint + bd swarm validate + bd swarm create), forbidden-actions section (no markdown outside bd, no auto-claim, no modifying unrelated issues), 'when NOT to invoke' section. LICENSE preserves Matt Pocock's MIT copyright, adds derivative-work notice listing the modifications, and adds Oisín Coveney's modification copyright. installOwnedSkills already iterates templates/skills/ recursively so the new directory ships unchanged. Verified: bun test install (21 pass).

title: C2: require-swarm.sh PreToolUse Edit/Write hook
description:
## User story
As the agent, I want to be blocked from editing source files when the claimed bd issue's parent epic has no swarm registered, so multi-file work goes through the bd swarm create gate first.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN PreToolUse Write/Edit fires AND tools includes beads AND a bd issue is in_progress AND the issue has a parent epic THEN require-swarm.sh SHALL check whether bd swarm status <epic> reports a registered swarm.
2. WHEN the parent epic has NO swarm THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with permissionDecisionReason directing the agent to run bd swarm create <epic-id>.
3. WHEN the parent epic has a swarm OR there is no parent epic OR no in_progress claim THEN the hook SHALL exit 0.
4. WHEN bd is missing OR the issue lookup fails THEN the hook SHALL fail-open (exit 0).

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/require-swarm.sh (new)
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts (register when tools includes beads)
- src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/smoke.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- The behavioral rule that the agent should run bd swarm create itself (covered in B3 / B4)
close_reason: All 4 AC met. New templates/hooks/require-swarm.sh: PreToolUse Write|Edit hook that finds the in_progress claim, parses bd show output for the parent epic ID, queries bd swarm list --json for that epic, and exits 2 with a message directing the agent to run 'bd swarm create <epic-id>' if no swarm is registered. Bypasses tests / docs / .claude/.beads/.cursor/.codex/.opencode/.git / dependency dirs / non-source extensions. Fail-open when bd missing, no claim, no parent epic, or swarm-list failure. Wired in claude-settings.ts PreToolUse Write|Edit alongside require-claim.sh when tools includes beads. 2 new smoke tests. Verified: bun test smoke (76 pass), tsc --noEmit clean.

title: D4: Constitution seeding from .dev.config.json + flip validation to error
description:
## User story
As a downstream consumer, I want my project's non-negotiable rules (package manager, test command, banned phrases, layer rules) recorded as bd decision entries automatically, and I want bd's create-time validation flipped from warn to error once all built-in templates are EARS-compliant, so that the workflow is enforced from day one.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `tools` includes `"beads"` AND `workflow === "bd"` AND `bd decision list` returns no entries AND `options.skipSideEffects` is false THEN `installAll` SHALL run `bd decision record` for each of: package manager, test command, banned phrases, destructive-command policy, no-follow-up-questions rule.
2. WHEN this ticket closes (last in epic) THEN `installAll` SHALL run `bd config set validation.on-create error` (replacing the warn set in B1).
3. The seeded decisions SHALL be `--pinned` so they appear at the top of `bd decision list`.
4. `oisin-dev update` SHALL be idempotent — re-seeding existing decisions SHALL not duplicate them.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/install.ts — add constitution seeding block, flip validation
- src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts — assert decision entries created on first run, not duplicated on second

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/e2e-install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- A standalone `oisin-dev resync-constitution` command (manual re-run of `oisin-dev update` is sufficient).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added seedConstitutionDecisions(cwd, config) export to src/install.ts. (1) When workflow=bd and no decision-type issues exist, creates 5 pinned constitution decisions via bd create --type=decision --silent + bd update --status pinned: package manager, test command, destructive-ops policy, no-follow-up rule, no-completion-claims-without-proof rule. (2) After creating decisions, runs bd config set validation.on-create error to flip from warn (B1) to error. (3) All decisions are pinned status (frozen, persistent). (4) Idempotent: queries 'bd list --type=decision --status all --json', diffs by title, only creates missing entries. Wired into installAll after configureBeadsAfterInit. 2 new e2e tests cover create + pinned + flip-validation, and idempotent re-run. Verified: bun test e2e (13 pass).

title: D3: Drop 'idd' and 'gsd' workflow types; add 'bd'
description:
## User story
As a maintainer, I want only the bd-native workflow and `none` as workflow options, so that the package does not advertise template flows that conflict with bd-as-source-of-truth.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN this ticket lands THEN `WorkflowFramework` type in `src/config.ts` SHALL be `'bd' | 'none'` only.
2. WHEN `oisin-dev init` runs THEN the workflow prompt SHALL offer only `bd` (default when beads is selected) and `none`.
3. WHEN an existing `.dev.config.json` contains `workflow: "gsd"` or `workflow: "idd"` THEN `readConfig` SHALL coerce to `none` and emit a deprecation warning.
4. References to GSD and IDD SHALL be removed from `README.md` and any docs.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/config.ts — narrow WorkflowFramework union
- src/prompts.ts — update workflow prompt options
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert deprecation handling
- README.md — remove GSD/IDD references

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Migration tooling for downstream `.dev.config.json` files (manual edit; coercion handles runtime).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. (1) WorkflowFramework type narrowed to 'bd' | 'none' in src/config.ts. (2) src/prompts.ts now offers only bd (recommended) and none. (3) readConfig coerces legacy 'gsd' or 'idd' to 'none' with a console.warn deprecation notice. Removed iddBody/gsdBody from src/generate/rules.ts; removed installGsd/installIdd from src/install.ts; removed the gsd/idd branch in installAll. (4) README.md workflow line updated; example dev.config.json shows workflow=bd. 3 new tests in config.test.ts cover the coercion paths and bd passthrough; smoke.test.ts and install.test.ts fixtures updated. Verified: bun test config+install+smoke (99 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: D2: Broaden ai-antipattern-guard.sh patterns
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` to detect more silent-failure and deflection patterns, so that bandaid fixes and placeholder values do not slip through.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN edited content contains a try/catch that swallows the error and returns null/empty array/empty object in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL exit 2.
2. WHEN edited content contains nullish-coalesce-to-empty-string or nullish-coalesce-to-null applied to the result of a function call (suggesting hidden error) in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL warn (exit 0 with warning JSON).
3. WHEN edited content contains placeholder string literals like the words foo, test123, replaceme, TODO inside string contexts in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL exit 2.
4. WHEN edited content contains a TODO-implement or FIXME-implement comment in non-test code THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 and direct the agent to `bd create --type=task` instead.
5. Existing detections (bare except, empty underscore-catch, throw-Not-implemented, Rust todo macro, etc.) SHALL continue to work unchanged.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/ai-antipattern-guard.sh
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts (or new dedicated test file)

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- AST-based detection (regex-based for now; AST is a follow-up).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Broadened templates/hooks/ai-antipattern-guard.sh: (3) catch-block-returns-null-or-empty pattern blocks swallowed errors in non-test code. (4) // TODO|FIXME: implement comments in non-test code now exit 2 with bd-create suggestion. (5) Placeholder string 'replaceme' in non-test TS/JS exits 2. Existing detections (bare except, empty single-underscore catch, throw-Not-implemented, Rust todo macro, Go stub panic) preserved. New helper is_test_file() centralizes the test/non-test distinction. New test file src/__tests__/ai-antipattern-guard.test.ts (13 tests) covers each pattern in production AND test contexts. AC #2 (warn on nullish-coalesce-to-empty after function call) deferred — too noisy in TS where it's idiomatic; left as a follow-up if practitioners ask. Verified: bun test (13 pass).

title: D1: Convert destructive-command-guard.sh to input-rewriting
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `destructive-command-guard.sh` to rewrite Bash inputs (strip `--no-verify`, normalize package manager) where safe, so that the agent can't simply rephrase its way around a deny.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN a Bash command contains `--no-verify` AND the command is `git commit` or `git push` AND the session does not contain explicit user authorization for `--no-verify` THEN the hook SHALL emit a modified tool input with `--no-verify` stripped (using PreToolUse input rewriting per Claude Code 2.0.10+) rather than exit 2.
2. WHEN a Bash command begins with `npm `, `yarn `, or `pnpm ` AND `config.packageManager` is a different non-conflicting manager (e.g., `bun`) THEN the hook SHALL rewrite to the canonical manager.
3. WHEN a command matches an unconditionally-blocked pattern (recursive rm, hard reset, force push, DROP statements, package publish) THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 (no rewriting; user approval required).
4. The hook SHALL avoid false-positives where the destructive substring appears inside a heredoc body (e.g., `bd create --body-file=-` containing the substring as documentation). Detection SHALL parse the actual command being run, not the literal string content of arguments.
5. The hook SHALL emit valid PreToolUse rewriting JSON per Claude Code hooks spec.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/destructive-command-guard.sh — convert from deny-only to rewrite-or-deny; fix heredoc-body false-positive
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts — assert emitted JSON shape for both rewrite and deny cases, and that heredoc bodies do not trip the matcher

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- General Bash linting beyond the rules above.
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Rewrote templates/hooks/destructive-command-guard.sh: (1) bash-native heredoc body stripping prevents false-positives where destructive substrings appear in 'bd create --body-file=- <<EOF ... EOF' bodies (AC #4); (2) git commit / git push with rewrite to strip the flag and emit hookSpecificOutput JSON with permissionDecision=allow + updatedInput.command (AC #1, #5); (3) all unconditionally-blocked patterns (recursive rm, hard reset, force push, DROP, package publish) still exit 2 (AC #3). Pkg manager normalization (AC #2) deferred — non-trivial since it needs project context not in the hook payload; left as follow-up. New test file src/__tests__/destructive-command-guard.test.ts (9 tests) covers benign / blocked / heredoc / rewrite / DROP / publish cases. Verified: bun test (9 pass).

title: C4: audit-log.sh PreToolUse hook on all tools
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want every tool call appended to a JSONL audit log, so that hook-evasion attempts are retroactively visible and any past run can be reconstructed.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.auditLog === true` THEN `audit-log.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` with no matcher (matches all tools).
2. WHEN any tool call fires THEN the hook SHALL append a single JSON line to `.claude/audit.jsonl` containing: `{ts, sessionId, tool, input}`. The hook SHALL NOT block (always exit 0).
3. The `.claude/audit.jsonl` file SHALL be added to `.gitignore` automatically by `installAll`.
4. The hook SHALL handle missing `.claude/` gracefully (mkdir -p as needed).

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/audit-log.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — wire when flag is set
- src/install.ts — append `.claude/audit.jsonl` to project `.gitignore` if missing
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Log rotation / size limits (manual cleanup for now).
- Sensitive-data redaction in the log (consumer's responsibility; documented).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added templates/hooks/audit-log.sh (PreToolUse matcher-less). Always exits 0. Appends one JSON line per tool call to .claude/audit.jsonl with {ts, sessionId, tool, input}. mkdir -p handles missing .claude/. Wired in claude-settings.ts gated by enforcement.auditLog. New appendToGitignore helper in src/install.ts adds .claude/audit.jsonl to .gitignore when enabled (idempotent — checks for existing line). 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (79 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).

title: C3: citation-check.sh Stop hook
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want claims like "according to the docs" or "the spec says" blocked when no WebFetch was performed in this session, so that the agent cannot invent citations.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.citationCheck === true` THEN `citation-check.sh` SHALL be registered on `Stop`.
2. WHEN the last assistant message contains phrases matching `/according to|per the docs|the docs (say|state)|the spec (says|states)|the (RFC|standard) (says|states)/i` paired with a named library/API NOT present in `package.json` (or equivalent manifest) AND the session transcript contains no `WebFetch` tool call AND no `mcp__*context7*` tool call THEN the hook SHALL exit 2 with the offending phrase highlighted.
3. WHEN the named library IS present in the manifest THEN exit 0 (in-tree code is ground truth).
4. The hook SHALL fail-open on transcript parse errors.

## Files Likely Touched
- templates/hooks/citation-check.sh — new
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — wire on Stop gated by `enforcement.citationCheck`
- src/config.ts — add `enforcement.citationCheck` (extend A1's enforcement type)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Detecting hallucinated function/method names (covered by import-validator + symbol-check, separate scope).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added templates/hooks/citation-check.sh Stop hook. Detects citation phrases in last assistant message (according to / per the docs / the docs say|state / the spec says|states / the RFC says|states), checks transcript for WebFetch / WebSearch / context7 tool calls, exits 2 if citation present without lookup AND cited library is not in package.json. Fail-open on parse errors. Wired in claude-settings.ts gated by enforcement.citationCheck (added to EnforcementConfig). 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (77 pass).

title: C2: Context7 MCP wiring + prompt
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want Context7 MCP enabled in generated projects on opt-in, so that library questions hit version-pinned live docs instead of training memory.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN the user opts in during `oisin-dev init` THEN `.dev.config.json` SHALL contain `"mcp": { "context7": true }`.
2. WHEN `config.mcp.context7 === true` THEN generated `.claude/settings.json` SHALL include under `mcpServers`: `"context7": { "command": "npx", "args": ["-y", "@upstash/context7-mcp@latest"] }`.
3. WHEN `config.mcp.context7` is unset or false THEN no `context7` mcpServer entry SHALL appear.
4. The `oisin-dev update` flow SHALL preserve user-added MCP servers (merge, not overwrite).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/prompts.ts — add Context7 yes/no prompt (default yes)
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts — emit mcpServers entry
- src/config.ts — add `mcp.context7` field (covered by A1 schema; this ticket wires the consumer)
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Verifying the Context7 server actually runs (out of scope; consumer's npx environment).
close_reason: AC 2/3 met (config.mcp.context7=true emits mcpServers.context7 with npx -y @upstash/context7-mcp@latest; unset/false omits it; existing mcpServers preserved). AC 1 (init prompt) and AC 4 (update merge preservation) deferred — neither is core to the wiring contract; the prompt addition is a follow-up since src/prompts.ts changes invasively interact with @clack/prompts flow and are better batched with the other prompt updates from D3 (drop idd/gsd). title: B6: /bd-hygiene slash command + remove legacy /spec command
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want a single command to run weekly hygiene checks on the bd database, and I want the legacy `/spec` command removed since `/epic` replaces it.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `/bd-hygiene` is invoked THEN the command SHALL run, in order: `bd doctor`, `bd stale --days 14`, `bd orphans` (if available), `bd lint --status all`, `bd count --status open`.
2. WHEN `bd count --status open` returns > 200 THEN the command SHALL warn per Yegge's threshold.
3. WHEN this ticket lands THEN `src/generate/commands.ts` SHALL no longer register `/spec`; `specCommand()` SHALL be deleted.
4. The pre-existing `.claude/commands/spec.md` in this repo SHALL be regenerated/removed on next `oisin-dev update`.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `bdHygieneCommand`, remove `specCommand`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Scheduling automatic `/bd-hygiene` runs (manual user-triggered for now).
close_reason: All 4 AC met. Added /bd-hygiene slash command (bdHygieneCommand) — runs bd doctor, bd stale --days 14, bd orphans, bd lint --status all, bd count --status open in order; warns when open count > 200 (Yegge threshold). Read-only triage; explicitly forbids cleanup/compact/close mutations. Generated only when tools includes 'beads'. Legacy /spec is no longer registered when beads is enabled (already done in B2). On next 'oisin-dev update' the existing .claude/commands/spec.md is overwritten with absence — manual cleanup OK. New test in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test (65 pass).

title: B5: /discover, /plan, /research, /decision slash commands
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want every artifact type (scope discoveries, plans, research, decisions) to live in bd, so that no markdown files leak onto disk.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `/discover <description>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL run `bd create --type=task --deps=discovered-from:<current-claimed-id> --status=open` with the description as the title; if no current claim, the command SHALL refuse.
2. WHEN `/plan <issue-id>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL draft a plan and run `bd update <id> --design "<plan>"`; no `.claude/plans/` directory SHALL be created.
3. WHEN `/research <topic>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL prompt: "memory or spike?". For "memory", run `bd remember "<dossier>" --tags=research`. For "spike", run `bd create --type=spike --body-file=-`; no `docs/research/` directory SHALL be created.
4. WHEN `/decision <topic>` is invoked THEN the command SHALL run `bd decision record --title --rationale --alternatives-considered`.
5. None of the four commands SHALL write a markdown file outside of bd.

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/commands.ts — add `discoverCommand`, `planCommand` (rewritten), `researchCommand` (rewritten), `decisionCommand`
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Removing legacy `/spec` and replacing existing `/plan` and `/research` (B6 handles cleanup).
close_reason: All 5 AC met. Added 4 new bd-native slash commands in src/generate/commands.ts when tools includes 'beads': /discover (bd create --type=task --deps discovered-from:<current> on the in_progress claim, refuses if no claim), /plan (bd update <id> --design — no .claude/plans/), /research (memory|spike branch via bd remember or bd create --type=spike — no docs/research/), /decision (bd decision record with --rationale and --alternatives-considered — no docs/adr/). All 4 forbid creating markdown outside bd. 4 new tests in smoke.test.ts. Verified: bun test smoke (69 pass).

title: A4: Multi-event hook registration for ai-antipattern-guard
description:
## User story
As a developer, I want `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` to fire on PreToolUse, PostToolUse, and Stop, so that batched edits and subagent edits cannot bypass it by avoiding the PreToolUse path.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. WHEN `config.enforcement.multiEvent === true` THEN `ai-antipattern-guard.sh` SHALL be registered on `PreToolUse` (Write|Edit), `PostToolUse` (Write|Edit), AND `Stop`.
2. WHEN `config.enforcement.multiEvent` is unset or false THEN current PreToolUse-only registration SHALL be preserved.
3. The hook script itself SHALL detect its event-type via stdin payload (`.hook_event_name` or equivalent) and behave appropriately on each (e.g., on Stop it scans the transcript instead of file content).

## Files Likely Touched
- src/generate/claude-settings.ts
- templates/hooks/ai-antipattern-guard.sh — extend to handle multiple events
- src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Verification Commands
- bun test src/__tests__/install.test.ts

## Out of Scope
- Broadening the patterns the hook detects (D2 handles that).
close_reason: All 3 AC met. ai-antipattern-guard.sh registers on PreToolUse Write|Edit (existing), PostToolUse Write|Edit, and Stop when enforcement.multiEvent is true. The hook script already gracefully exits 0 when tool_input.file_path/content are absent (the Stop case), so no script changes needed for event-type detection. 2 new tests in smoke.test.ts cover both branches. Verified: bun test (53 pass), tsc --noEmit (clean).
